title: Caloncoba gilgiana
intro: Caloncoba gilgiana, also known as the African dogwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in African medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Caloncoba gilgiana include flavonoids, tannins, and alkaloids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Caloncoba gilgiana is often used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Caloncoba gilgiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae, commonly known as the Chrysobalanaceae family, however this name is sometimes disputed, and some sources refer to the family as Ochnaceae. It belongs to the order Malpighiales and is native to tropical Africa. This plant is part of the genus Caloncoba which consists of several species of trees and shrubs. The classification of Caloncoba gilgiana is further refined into the kingdom Plantae. The exact classification of this plant may vary depending on the source.

The plant is said to be used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
